title: state_law
description: One row per U.S. state. Carries public-records-law facts that are jurisdiction-level (not program-level). See pipeline/workflow_design_V2.md §5.4. Schema is generic across all 50 states; only MA is populated in V2.

name.description: Common name of the law, e.g. 'Massachusetts Public Records Law'.

citation.description: Primary statutory citation for access/response procedures, e.g. 'M.G.L. c. 66, § 10'.

response_deadline.description: Primary response deadline in form 'N business days' or 'N calendar days'.

response_deadline_citation.description: Statute subsection holding the response deadline, e.g. 'M.G.L. c. 66, § 10(a)'. Added in V2 §5.4 update post-1a annotation.

extension.description: Structured replacement for V2 §5.4's single extension_clause string. Captures multi-stage extensions like MA's agency-initial + Supervisor-granted scheme. Any field may be null when not applicable.

judicial_venue.description: Judicial-review venue distinct from administrative appeal venue. Added in V2 §5.4 update post-1a annotation.

record_definition.description: Paraphrase of the statutory definition of 'public record'.

record_definition_citation.description: Statute citation for the 'public record' definition, e.g. 'M.G.L. c. 4, § 7, cl. 26'. Added in V2 §5.4 update post-1a annotation.

segregation_clause.description: Statute or paraphrase of the rule that exempt portions can be withheld but non-exempt portions of the same record must still be produced.

residency_restriction.description: True if the law limits requests to in-state residents.

oral_requests_allowed.description: True if the law allows oral (in-person or phone) public records requests. New in V2 schema post-1c. Note: oral requests may not be sufficient for appeal.

branch_carveouts.description: Branches/entities the Public Records Law does not apply to (e.g., legislature, courts, federal agencies). New in V2 schema post-1c.

field.description: Dotted path of the field this evidence supports, e.g. 'public_records_law.citation'.

source_ref.description: URL, local file path, or production package document id.

quote.description: Verbatim span from the source supporting the field value.
